title: Davallia tyermannii
intro: Davallia tyermannii, also known as the New Zealand fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Davallia tyermannii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Davallia tyermannii is classified as a species of fern in the family Davalliaceae. It belongs to the genus Davallia, which includes several species of ferns. Davallia tyermannii is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Davallia tyermannii is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification makes it a part of the larger group of vascular plants.
